NKJV.1: There was a man of the Pharisees named Nicodemus, a ruler of the Jews.
NKJV.2: This man came to Jesus by night and said to Him, “Rabbi, we know that You are a teacher come from God; for no one can do these signs that You do unless God is with him.”
NKJV.3: Jesus answered and said to him, “Most assuredly, I say to you,  unless one is born again, he cannot see the kingdom of God.”
NKJV.4: Nicodemus said to Him, “How can a man be born when he is old? Can he enter a second time into his mother’s womb and be born?”
NKJV.5: Jesus answered, “Most assuredly, I say to you,  unless one is born of water and the Spirit, he cannot enter the kingdom of God.
NKJV.6: That which is born of the flesh is  flesh, and that which is born of the Spirit is spirit.
NKJV.7: Do not marvel that I said to you, ‘You must be born again.’
NKJV.8: The wind blows where it wishes, and you hear the sound of it, but cannot tell where it comes from and where it goes. So is everyone who is born of the Spirit.”
NKJV.9: Nicodemus answered and said to Him, “How can these things be?”
NKJV.10: Jesus answered and said to him, “Are you the teacher of Israel, and do not know these things?
NKJV.11: Most assuredly, I say to you, We speak what We know and testify what We have seen, and  you do not receive Our witness.
NKJV.12: If I have told you earthly things and you do not believe, how will you believe if I tell you heavenly things?
NKJV.13: No one has ascended to heaven but He who came down from heaven, that is, the Son of Man who is in heaven.
NKJV.14: And as Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, even so  must the Son of Man be lifted up,
NKJV.15: that whoever  believes in Him should not perish but have eternal life.
NKJV.16: For God so loved the world that He gave His only begotten  Son, that whoever believes in Him should not perish but have everlasting life.
NKJV.17: For God did not send His Son into the world to condemn the world, but that the world through Him might be saved.
NKJV.18: “He who believes in Him is not condemned; but he who does not believe is condemned already, because he has not believed in the name of the only begotten Son of God.
NKJV.19: And this is the condemnation,  that the light has come into the world, and men loved darkness rather than light, because their deeds were evil.
NKJV.20: For  everyone practicing evil hates the light and does not come to the light, lest his deeds should be exposed.
NKJV.21: But he who does the truth comes to the light, that his deeds may be clearly seen, that they have been  done in God.”
NKJV.22: After these things Jesus and His disciples came into the land of Judea, and there He remained with them and baptized.
NKJV.23: Now John also was baptizing in Aenon near Salim, because there was much water there. And they came and were baptized.
NKJV.24: For John had not yet been thrown into prison.
NKJV.25: Then there arose a dispute between some of John’s disciples and the Jews about purification.
NKJV.26: And they came to John and said to him, “Rabbi, He who was with you beyond the Jordan, to whom you have testified—behold, He is baptizing, and all are coming to Him!”
NKJV.27: John answered and said, “A man can receive nothing unless it has been given to him from heaven.
NKJV.28: You yourselves bear me witness, that I said, ‘I am not the Christ,’ but, ‘I have been sent before Him.’
NKJV.29: He who has the bride is the bridegroom; but the friend of the bridegroom, who stands and hears him, rejoices greatly because of the bridegroom’s voice. Therefore this joy of mine is fulfilled.
NKJV.30: He must increase, but I must decrease.
NKJV.31: He who comes from above is above all; he who is of the earth is earthly and speaks of the earth. He who comes from heaven is above all.
NKJV.32: And what He has seen and heard, that He testifies; and no one receives His testimony.
NKJV.33: He who has received His testimony has certified that God is true.
NKJV.34: For He whom God has sent speaks the words of God, for God does not give the Spirit by measure.
NKJV.35: The Father loves the Son, and has given all things into His hand.
NKJV.36: He who believes in the Son has everlasting life; and he who does not believe the Son shall not see life, but the wrath of God abides on him.”

ESV.1: Now there was a man of the Pharisees named Nicodemus, a ruler of the Jews.
ESV.2: This man came to Jesus  by night and said to him, “Rabbi, we know that you are a teacher come from God, for no one can do these signs that you do unless God is with him.”
ESV.3: Jesus answered him, “Truly, truly, I say to you, unless one is born again  he cannot see the kingdom of God.”
ESV.4: Nicodemus said to him, “How can a man be born when he is old? Can he enter a second time into his mother's womb and be born?”
ESV.5: Jesus answered, “Truly, truly, I say to you, unless one is born of water and the Spirit, he cannot enter the kingdom of God.
ESV.6: That which is born of the flesh is flesh, and that which is born of the Spirit is spirit.
ESV.7: Do not marvel that I said to you, ‘You  must be born again.’
ESV.8: The wind  blows where it wishes, and you hear its sound, but you do not know where it comes from or where it goes. So it is with everyone who is born of the Spirit.”
ESV.9: Nicodemus said to him, “How can these things be?”
ESV.10: Jesus answered him, “Are you the teacher of Israel and yet you do not understand these things?
ESV.11: Truly, truly, I say to you, we speak of what we know, and bear witness to what we have seen, but you  do not receive our testimony.
ESV.12: If I have told you earthly things and you do not believe, how can you believe if I tell you heavenly things?
ESV.13: No one has ascended into heaven except he who descended from heaven, the Son of Man.
ESV.14: And as Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, so must the Son of Man be lifted up,
ESV.15: that whoever believes in him may have eternal life.
ESV.16: “For God so loved the world,  that he gave his only Son, that whoever believes in him should not perish but have eternal life.
ESV.17: For God did not send his Son into the world to condemn the world, but in order that the world might be saved through him.
ESV.18: Whoever believes in him is not condemned, but whoever does not believe is condemned already, because he has not believed in the name of the only Son of God.
ESV.19: And this is the judgment: the light has come into the world, and people loved the darkness rather than the light because their works were evil.
ESV.20: For everyone who does wicked things hates the light and does not come to the light, lest his works should be exposed.
ESV.21: But whoever does what is true comes to the light, so that it may be clearly seen that his works have been carried out in God.”
ESV.22: After this Jesus and his disciples went into the Judean countryside, and he remained there with them and was baptizing.
ESV.23: John also was baptizing at Aenon near Salim, because water was plentiful there, and people were coming and being baptized
ESV.24: (for John had not yet been put in prison).
ESV.25: Now a discussion arose between some of John's disciples and a Jew over purification.
ESV.26: And they came to John and said to him, “Rabbi, he who was with you across the Jordan, to whom you bore witness — look, he is baptizing, and all are going to him.”
ESV.27: John answered, “A person cannot receive even one thing unless it is given him from heaven.
ESV.28: You yourselves bear me witness, that I said, ‘I am not the Christ, but I have been sent before him.’
ESV.29: The one who has the bride is the bridegroom. The friend of the bridegroom, who stands and hears him, rejoices greatly at the bridegroom's voice. Therefore this joy of mine is now complete.
ESV.30: He must increase, but I must decrease.”
ESV.31: He who comes from above is above all. He who is of the earth belongs to the earth and speaks in an earthly way. He who comes from heaven is above all.
ESV.32: He bears witness to what he has seen and heard, yet no one receives his testimony.
ESV.33: Whoever receives his testimony sets his seal to this, that God is true.
ESV.34: For he whom God has sent utters the words of God, for he gives the Spirit without measure.
ESV.35: The Father loves the Son and has given all things into his hand.
ESV.36: Whoever believes in the Son has eternal life; whoever does not obey the Son shall not see life, but the wrath of God remains on him.

NASB.1: Now there was a man of the Pharisees, named Nicodemus, a ruler of the Jews;
NASB.2: this man came to Jesus by night and said to Him, 'Rabbi, we know that You have come from God [as] a teacher; for no one can do these signs that You do unless God is with him.'
NASB.3: Jesus answered and said to him, 'Truly, truly, I say to you, unless one is born again he cannot see the kingdom of God.'
NASB.4: Nicodemus said to Him, 'How can a man be born when he is old? He cannot enter a second time into his mother's womb and be born, can he?'
NASB.5: Jesus answered, 'Truly, truly, I say to you, unless one is born of water and the Spirit he cannot enter into the kingdom of God.
NASB.6: 'That which is born of the flesh is flesh, and that which is born of the Spirit is spirit.
NASB.7: 'Do not be amazed that I said to you, 'You must be born again.'
NASB.8: 'The wind blows where it wishes and you hear the sound of it, but do not know where it comes from and where it is going; so is everyone who is born of the Spirit.'
NASB.9: Nicodemus said to Him, 'How can these things be?'
NASB.10: Jesus answered and said to him, 'Are you the teacher of Israel and do not understand these things?
NASB.11: 'Truly, truly, I say to you, we speak of what we know and testify of what we have seen, and you do not accept our testimony.
NASB.12: 'If I told you earthly things and you do not believe, how will you believe if I tell you heavenly things?
NASB.13: 'No one has ascended into heaven, but He who descended from heaven: the Son of Man.
NASB.14: 'As Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, even so must the Son of Man be lifted up;
NASB.15: so that whoever believes will in Him have eternal life.
NASB.16: 'For God so loved the world, that He gave His only begotten Son, that whoever believes in Him shall not perish, but have eternal life.
NASB.17: 'For God did not send the Son into the world to judge the world, but that the world might be saved through Him.
NASB.18: 'He who believes in Him is not judged; he who does not believe has been judged already, because he has not believed in the name of the only begotten Son of God.
NASB.19: 'This is the judgment, that the Light has come into the world, and men loved the darkness rather than the Light, for their deeds were evil.
NASB.20: 'For everyone who does evil hates the Light, and does not come to the Light for fear that his deeds will be exposed.
NASB.21: 'But he who practices the truth comes to the Light, so that his deeds may be manifested as having been wrought in God.'
NASB.22: After these things Jesus and His disciples came into the land of Judea, and there He was spending time with them and baptizing.
NASB.23: John also was baptizing in Aenon near Salim, because there was much water there; and [people] were coming and were being baptized--
NASB.24: for John had not yet been thrown into prison.
NASB.25: Therefore there arose a discussion on the part of John's disciples with a Jew about purification.
NASB.26: And they came to John and said to him, 'Rabbi, He who was with you beyond the Jordan, to whom you have testified, behold, He is baptizing and all are coming to Him.'
NASB.27: John answered and said, 'A man can receive nothing unless it has been given him from heaven.
NASB.28: 'You yourselves are my witnesses that I said, 'I am not the Christ,' but, 'I have been sent ahead of Him.'
NASB.29: 'He who has the bride is the bridegroom; but the friend of the bridegroom, who stands and hears him, rejoices greatly because of the bridegroom's voice. So this joy of mine has been made full.
NASB.30: 'He must increase, but I must decrease.
NASB.31: 'He who comes from above is above all, he who is of the earth is from the earth and speaks of the earth. He who comes from heaven is above all.
NASB.32: 'What He has seen and heard, of that He testifies; and no one receives His testimony.
NASB.33: 'He who has received His testimony has set his seal to [this], that God is true.
NASB.34: 'For He whom God has sent speaks the words of God; for He gives the Spirit without measure.
NASB.35: 'The Father loves the Son and has given all things into His hand.
NASB.36: 'He who believes in the Son has eternal life; but he who does not obey the Son will not see life, but the wrath of God abides on him.'

NLT.1: There was a man named Nicodemus, a Jewish religious leader who was a Pharisee.
NLT.2: After dark one evening, he came to speak with Jesus. “Rabbi,” he said, “we all know that God has sent you to teach us. Your miraculous signs are evidence that God is with you.”
NLT.3: Jesus replied, “I tell you the truth, unless you are born again, you cannot see the Kingdom of God.”
NLT.4: “What do you mean?” exclaimed Nicodemus. “How can an old man go back into his mother’s womb and be born again?”
NLT.5: Jesus replied, “I assure you, no one can enter the Kingdom of God without being born of water and the Spirit.
NLT.6: Humans can reproduce only human life, but the Holy Spirit gives birth to spiritual life.
NLT.7: So don’t be surprised when I say, ‘You must be born again.’
NLT.8: The wind blows wherever it wants. Just as you can hear the wind but can’t tell where it comes from or where it is going, so you can’t explain how people are born of the Spirit.”
NLT.9: “How are these things possible?” Nicodemus asked.
NLT.10: Jesus replied, “You are a respected Jewish teacher, and yet you don’t understand these things?
NLT.11: I assure you, we tell you what we know and have seen, and yet you won’t believe our testimony.
NLT.12: But if you don’t believe me when I tell you about earthly things, how can you possibly believe if I tell you about heavenly things?
NLT.13: No one has ever gone to heaven and returned. But the Son of Man has come down from heaven.
NLT.14: And as Moses lifted up the bronze snake on a pole in the wilderness, so the Son of Man must be lifted up,
NLT.15: so that everyone who believes in him will have eternal life.
NLT.16: “For this is how God loved the world: He gave his one and only Son, so that everyone who believes in him will not perish but have eternal life.
NLT.17: God sent his Son into the world not to judge the world, but to save the world through him.
NLT.18: “There is no judgment against anyone who believes in him. But anyone who does not believe in him has already been judged for not believing in God’s one and only Son.
NLT.19: And the judgment is based on this fact: God’s light came into the world, but people loved the darkness more than the light, for their actions were evil.
NLT.20: All who do evil hate the light and refuse to go near it for fear their sins will be exposed.
NLT.21: But those who do what is right come to the light so others can see that they are doing what God wants. ”
NLT.22: Then Jesus and his disciples left Jerusalem and went into the Judean countryside. Jesus spent some time with them there, baptizing people.
NLT.23: At this time John the Baptist was baptizing at Aenon, near Salim, because there was plenty of water there; and people kept coming to him for baptism.
NLT.24: (This was before John was thrown into prison.)
NLT.25: A debate broke out between John’s disciples and a certain Jew over ceremonial cleansing.
NLT.26: So John’s disciples came to him and said, “Rabbi, the man you met on the other side of the Jordan River, the one you identified as the Messiah, is also baptizing people. And everybody is going to him instead of coming to us.”
NLT.27: John replied, “No one can receive anything unless God gives it from heaven.
NLT.28: You yourselves know how plainly I told you, ‘I am not the Messiah. I am only here to prepare the way for him.’
NLT.29: It is the bridegroom who marries the bride, and the bridegroom’s friend is simply glad to stand with him and hear his vows. Therefore, I am filled with joy at his success.
NLT.30: He must become greater and greater, and I must become less and less.
NLT.31: “He has come from above and is greater than anyone else. We are of the earth, and we speak of earthly things, but he has come from heaven and is greater than anyone else.
NLT.32: He testifies about what he has seen and heard, but how few believe what he tells them!
NLT.33: Anyone who accepts his testimony can affirm that God is true.
NLT.34: For he is sent by God. He speaks God’s words, for God gives him the Spirit without limit.
NLT.35: The Father loves his Son and has put everything into his hands.
NLT.36: And anyone who believes in God’s Son has eternal life. Anyone who doesn’t obey the Son will never experience eternal life but remains under God’s angry judgment.”

WEB.1: Now there was a man of the Pharisees named Nicodemus, a ruler of the Jews.
WEB.2: The same came to him by night, and said to him, “Rabbi, we know that you are a teacher come from God, for no one can do these signs that you do, unless God is with him.”
WEB.3: Jesus answered him, “Most certainly, I tell you, unless one is born anew,   he can’t see God’s Kingdom.”
WEB.4: Nicodemus said to him, “How can a man be born when he is old? Can he enter a second time into his mother’s womb, and be born?”
WEB.5: Jesus answered, “Most certainly I tell you, unless one is born of water and spirit, he can’t enter into God’s Kingdom.
WEB.6: That which is born of the flesh is flesh. That which is born of the Spirit is spirit.
WEB.7: Don’t marvel that I said to you, ‘You must be born anew.’
WEB.8: The wind  blows where it wants to, and you hear its sound, but don’t know where it comes from and where it is going. So is everyone who is born of the Spirit.”
WEB.9: Nicodemus answered him, “How can these things be?”
WEB.10: Jesus answered him, “Are you the teacher of Israel, and don’t understand these things?
WEB.11: Most certainly I tell you, we speak that which we know, and testify of that which we have seen, and you don’t receive our witness.
WEB.12: If I told you earthly things and you don’t believe, how will you believe if I tell you heavenly things?
WEB.13: No one has ascended into heaven, but he who descended out of heaven, the Son of Man, who is in heaven.
WEB.14: As Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, even so must the Son of Man be lifted up,
WEB.15: that whoever believes in him should not perish, but have eternal life.
WEB.16: For God so loved the world, that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him should not perish, but have eternal life.
WEB.17: For God didn’t send his Son into the world to judge the world, but that the world should be saved through him.
WEB.18: He who believes in him is not judged. He who doesn’t believe has been judged already, because he has not believed in the name of the one and only Son of God.
WEB.19: This is the judgment, that the light has come into the world, and men loved the darkness rather than the light; for their works were evil.
WEB.20: For everyone who does evil hates the light, and doesn’t come to the light, lest his works would be exposed.
WEB.21: But he who does the truth comes to the light, that his works may be revealed, that they have been done in God.”
WEB.22: After these things, Jesus came with his disciples into the land of Judea. He stayed there with them, and baptized.
WEB.23: John also was baptizing in Enon near Salim, because there was much water there. They came, and were baptized.
WEB.24: For John was not yet thrown into prison.
WEB.25: There arose therefore a questioning on the part of John’s disciples with some Jews about purification.
WEB.26: They came to John, and said to him, “Rabbi, he who was with you beyond the Jordan, to whom you have testified, behold, the same baptizes, and everyone is coming to him.”
WEB.27: John answered, “A man can receive nothing, unless it has been given him from heaven.
WEB.28: You yourselves testify that I said, ‘I am not the Christ,’ but, ‘I have been sent before him.’
WEB.29: He who has the bride is the bridegroom; but the friend of the bridegroom, who stands and hears him, rejoices greatly because of the bridegroom’s voice. This, my joy, therefore is made full.
WEB.30: He must increase, but I must decrease.
WEB.31: He who comes from above is above all. He who is from the earth belongs to the earth, and speaks of the earth. He who comes from heaven is above all.
WEB.32: What he has seen and heard, of that he testifies; and no one receives his witness.
WEB.33: He who has received his witness has set his seal to this, that God is true.
WEB.34: For he whom God has sent speaks the words of God; for God gives the Spirit without measure.
WEB.35: The Father loves the Son, and has given all things into his hand.
WEB.36: One who believes in the Son has eternal life, but one who disobeys  the Son won’t see life, but the wrath of God remains on him.”

CSB17.1: There was a man from the Pharisees <sup>ⓒ</sup> named Nicodemus, <sup>ⓓ</sup> a ruler <sup>ⓔ</sup> of the Jews.
CSB17.2: This man came to him at night and said, “Rabbi, <sup>ⓕ</sup> we know that you are a teacher <sup>ⓖ</sup> who has come from God, <sup>ⓗ</sup> for no one could perform these signs you do unless God were with him.” <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.3: Jesus replied, “Truly I tell you, unless someone is born again, <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>ⓙ</sup>he cannot see the kingdom of God.” <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.4: “How can anyone be born when he is old?” Nicodemus asked him. “Can he enter his mother’s womb a second time and be born?”
CSB17.5: Jesus answered, “Truly I tell you, unless someone is born <sup>ⓛ</sup>of water and the Spirit, <sup>ⓜ</sup>he cannot enter the kingdom of God.
CSB17.6: Whatever is born of the flesh <sup>ⓝ</sup>is flesh, and whatever is born of the Spirit <sup>ⓐ</sup>is spirit.
CSB17.7: Do not be amazed that I told you that you must be born <sup>ⓑ</sup>again.
CSB17.8: The wind blows where it pleases, and you hear its sound, but you don’t know where it comes from or where it is going. <sup>ⓒ</sup>So it is with everyone born of the Spirit.” <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.9: “How can these things be?” asked Nicodemus.
CSB17.10: “Are you a teacher <sup>[2]</sup> of Israel and don’t know these things?” Jesus replied.
CSB17.11: “Truly I tell you, <sup>ⓔ</sup>we speak what we know and we testify to what we have seen, but you do not accept our testimony.
CSB17.12: If I have told you about earthly things and you don’t believe, how will you believe if I tell you about heavenly things?
CSB17.13: No one has ascended <sup>ⓕ</sup>into heaven <sup>ⓖ</sup>except the one who descended from heaven <sup>ⓗ</sup>— the Son of Man. <sup>[3]</sup> <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.14: “Just as Moses <sup>ⓙ</sup> lifted up the snake in the wilderness, <sup>ⓚ</sup> so the Son of Man must be lifted up,
CSB17.15: so that everyone who believes in him may <sup>[4]</sup>have eternal life. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.16: For God loved <sup>ⓜ</sup>the world in this way: <sup>[5]</sup>He gave <sup>[6]</sup>his one and only <sup>ⓝ</sup>Son, <sup>ⓐ</sup>so that everyone who believes in him will not perish but have eternal life. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.17: For God did not send his Son into the world to condemn <sup>ⓒ</sup>the world, but to save the world through him.
CSB17.18: Anyone who believes in him is not condemned, but anyone who does not believe is already condemned, <sup>ⓓ</sup>because he has not believed in the name <sup>ⓔ</sup>of the one and only Son <sup>ⓕ</sup>of God.
CSB17.19: This is the judgment: The light <sup>ⓖ</sup>has come into the world, <sup>ⓗ</sup>and people loved darkness rather than the light because their deeds were evil.
CSB17.20: For everyone who does evil hates <sup>ⓘ</sup> the light and avoids it, <sup>[7]</sup>so that his deeds <sup>ⓙ</sup>may not be exposed.
CSB17.21: But anyone who lives by <sup>[8]</sup>the truth comes to the light, so that his works <sup>ⓚ</sup>may be shown to be accomplished by God.”
CSB17.22: After this, Jesus and his disciples went to the Judean countryside, where he spent time with them and baptized.
CSB17.23: John <sup>ⓛ</sup> also was baptizing in Aenon near Salim, because there was plenty of water there. People were coming and being baptized,
CSB17.24: since John had not yet been thrown into prison. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.25: Then a dispute arose between John’s disciples <sup>ⓝ</sup> and a Jew <sup>[9]</sup> about purification. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.26: So they came to John and told him, “Rabbi, <sup>ⓑ</sup> the one you testified about, and who was with you across the Jordan, <sup>ⓒ</sup> is baptizing — and everyone is going to him.” <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.27: John responded, “No one can receive anything unless it has been given to him from heaven.
CSB17.28: You yourselves can testify that I said, ‘I am not the Messiah, <sup>ⓔ</sup> but I’ve been sent ahead of him.’ <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.29: He who has the bride <sup>ⓖ</sup> is the groom. But the groom’s friend, who stands by and listens for him, rejoices greatly <sup>[10]</sup> at the groom’s voice. So this joy <sup>ⓗ</sup> of mine is complete.
CSB17.30: He must increase, but I must decrease.”
CSB17.31: The one who comes from above <sup>ⓘ</sup> is above all. The one who is from the earth is earthly and speaks in earthly terms. <sup>[11]</sup> The one who comes from heaven is above all. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.32: He testifies <sup>ⓚ</sup> to what he has seen and heard, and yet no one accepts his testimony.
CSB17.33: The one who has accepted his testimony has affirmed that God is true. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.34: For the one whom God sent speaks God’s words, since he <sup>[12]</sup> gives the Spirit <sup>ⓜ</sup> without measure.
CSB17.35: The Father loves the Son <sup>ⓝ</sup> and has given all things into his hands. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.36: The one who believes in the Son has eternal life, but the one who rejects the Son <sup>[13]</sup> will not see life; instead, the wrath of God remains on him. <sup>ⓑ</sup>

AMP.1: Now there was a certain man among the Pharisees named Nicodemus, a ruler (member of the Sanhedrin) among the Jews,
AMP.2: who came to Jesus at night and said to Him, "Rabbi (Teacher), we know [without any doubt] that You have come from God as a teacher; for no one can do these signs [these wonders, these attesting miracles] that You do unless God is with him."
AMP.3: Jesus answered him, "I assure you and most solemnly say to you, unless a person is born again [reborn from above—spiritually transformed, renewed, sanctified], he cannot [ever] see and experience the kingdom of God."
AMP.4: Nicodemus said to Him, "How can a man be born when he is old? He cannot enter his mother's womb a second time and be born, can he?"
AMP.5: Jesus answered, "I assure you and most solemnly say to you, unless one is born of water and the Spirit he cannot [ever] enter the kingdom of God.
AMP.6: That which is born of the flesh is flesh [the physical is merely physical], and that which is born of the Spirit is spirit.
AMP.7: Do not be surprised that I have told you, 'You must be born again [reborn from above—spiritually transformed, renewed, sanctified].'
AMP.8: The wind blows where it wishes and you hear its sound, but you do not know where it is coming from and where it is going; so it is with everyone who is born of the Spirit."
AMP.9: Nicodemus said to Him, "How can these things be possible?"
AMP.10: Jesus replied, "You are the [great and well-known] teacher of Israel, and yet you do not know nor understand these things [from Scripture]?
AMP.11: I assure you and most solemnly say to you, we speak only of what we [absolutely] know and testify about what we have [actually] seen [as eyewitnesses]; and [still] you [reject our evidence and] do not accept our testimony.
AMP.12: If I told you earthly things [that is, things that happen right here on earth] and you do not believe, how will you believe and trust Me if I tell you heavenly things?
AMP.13: No one has gone up into heaven, but there is One who came down from heaven, the Son of Man [Himself—whose home is in heaven].
AMP.14: Just as Moses lifted up the [bronze] serpent in the desert [on a pole], so must the Son of Man be lifted up [on the cross],
AMP.15: so that whoever believes will in Him have eternal life [after physical death, and will actually live forever].
AMP.16: "For God so [greatly] loved and dearly prized the world, that He [even] gave His [One and] only begotten Son, so that whoever believes and trusts in Him [as Savior] shall not perish, but have eternal life.
AMP.17: For God did not send the Son into the world to judge and condemn the world [that is, to initiate the final judgment of the world], but that the world might be saved through Him.
AMP.18: Whoever believes and has decided to trust in Him [as personal Savior and Lord] is not judged [for this one, there is no judgment, no rejection, no condemnation]; but the one who does not believe [and has decided to reject Him as personal Savior and Lord] is judged already [that one has been convicted and sentenced], because he has not believed and trusted in the name of the [One and] only begotten Son of God [the One who is truly unique, the only One of His kind, the One who alone can save him].
AMP.19: This is the judgment [that is, the cause for indictment, the test by which people are judged, the basis for the sentence]: the Light has come into the world, and people loved the darkness rather than the Light, for their deeds were evil.
AMP.20: For every wrongdoer hates the Light, and does not come to the Light [but shrinks from it] for fear that his [sinful, worthless] activities will be exposed and condemned.
AMP.21: But whoever practices truth [and does what is right—morally, ethically, spiritually] comes to the Light, so that his works may be plainly shown to be what they are—accomplished in God [divinely prompted, done with God's help, in dependence on Him]."
AMP.22: After these things Jesus and His disciples went into the land of Judea, and there He spent time with them and baptized.
AMP.23: Now John was also baptizing at Aenon near Salim, because there was an abundance of water there; and people were coming and were being baptized—
AMP.24: for John had not yet been thrown into prison.
AMP.25: Therefore there arose a controversy between John's disciples and a Jew in regard to purification (ceremonial washing).
AMP.26: So they came to John and said to him, "Rabbi (Teacher), the Man who was with you on the other side of the Jordan [at the Jordan River crossing]—and to whom you have testified—look, He is baptizing too, and everyone is going to Him!"
AMP.27: John replied, "A man can receive nothing [he can claim nothing at all] unless it has been granted to him from heaven [for there is no other source than the sovereign will of God].
AMP.28: You yourselves are my witnesses that I stated, 'I am not the Christ (the Messiah, the Anointed),' but, 'I have [only] been sent ahead of Him [as His appointed forerunner and messenger to announce and proclaim His coming].'
AMP.29: He who has the bride is the bridegroom; but the friend of the bridegroom, who stands by and listens to him, rejoices greatly because of the bridegroom's voice. So this pleasure and joy of mine is now complete.
AMP.30: He must increase [in prominence], but I must decrease.
AMP.31: "He who comes from [heaven] above is above all others; he who is of the earth is from the earth and speaks [about things] of the earth [his viewpoint and experience are earthly]. He who comes from heaven is above all.
AMP.32: What He has [actually] seen and heard, of that He testifies; and yet no one accepts His testimony [as true].
AMP.33: Whoever receives His testimony has set his seal [of approval] to this: God is true [and he knows that God cannot lie].
AMP.34: For He whom God has sent speaks the words of God [proclaiming the Father's own message]; for God gives the [gift of the] Spirit without measure [generously and boundlessly]!
AMP.35: The Father loves the Son and has given and entrusted all things into His hand.
AMP.36: He who believes and trusts in the Son and accepts Him [as Savior] has eternal life [that is, already possesses it]; but he who does not believe the Son and chooses to reject Him, [disobeying Him and denying Him as Savior] will not see [eternal] life, but [instead] the wrath of God hangs over him continually."

MSG.1: There was a man of the Pharisee sect, Nicodemus, a prominent leader among the Jews.
MSG.2: Late one night he visited Jesus and said, "Rabbi, we all know you're a teacher straight from God. No one could do all the God-pointing, God-revealing acts you do if God weren't in on it."
MSG.3: Jesus said, "You're absolutely right. Take it from me: Unless a person is born from above, it's not possible to see what I'm pointing to—to God's kingdom."
MSG.4: "How can anyone," said Nicodemus, "be born who has already been born and grown up? You can't re-enter your mother's womb and be born again. What are you saying with this 'born-from-above' talk?"
MSG.5: Jesus said, "You're not listening. Let me say it again. Unless a person submits to this original creation—the 'wind hovering over the water' creation, the invisible moving the visible, a baptism into a new life—it's not possible to enter God's kingdom.
MSG.6: When you look at a baby, it's just that: a body you can look at and touch. But the person who takes shape within is formed by something you can't see and touch—the Spirit—and becomes a living spirit.
MSG.7: "So don't be so surprised when I tell you that you have to be 'born from above'—out of this world, so to speak.
MSG.8: You know well enough how the wind blows this way and that. You hear it rustling through the trees, but you have no idea where it comes from or where it's headed next. That's the way it is with everyone 'born from above' by the wind of God, the Spirit of God."
MSG.9: Nicodemus asked, "What do you mean by this? How does this happen?"
MSG.10: Jesus said, "You're a respected teacher of Israel and you don't know these basics?
MSG.11: Listen carefully. I'm speaking sober truth to you. I speak only of what I know by experience; I give witness only to what I have seen with my own eyes. There is nothing secondhand here, no hearsay. Yet instead of facing the evidence and accepting it, you procrastinate with questions.
MSG.12: If I tell you things that are plain as the hand before your face and you don't believe me, what use is there in telling you of things you can't see, the things of God?
MSG.13: "No one has ever gone up into the presence of God except the One who came down from that Presence, the Son of Man.
MSG.14: In the same way that Moses lifted the serpent in the desert so people could have something to see and then believe, it is necessary for the Son of Man to be lifted up—
MSG.15: and everyone who looks up to him, trusting and expectant, will gain a real life, eternal life.
MSG.16: "This is how much God loved the world: He gave his Son, his one and only Son. And this is why: so that no one need be destroyed; by believing in him, anyone can have a whole and lasting life.
MSG.17: God didn't go to all the trouble of sending his Son merely to point an accusing finger, telling the world how bad it was. He came to help, to put the world right again.
MSG.18: Anyone who trusts in him is acquitted; anyone who refuses to trust him has long since been under the death sentence without knowing it. And why? Because of that person's failure to believe in the one-of-a-kind Son of God when introduced to him.
MSG.19: "This is the crisis we're in: God-light streamed into the world, but men and women everywhere ran for the darkness. They went for the darkness because they were not really interested in pleasing God.
MSG.20: Everyone who makes a practice of doing evil, addicted to denial and illusion, hates God-light and won't come near it, fearing a painful exposure.
MSG.21: But anyone working and living in truth and reality welcomes God-light so the work can be seen for the God-work it is."
MSG.22: After this conversation, Jesus went on with his disciples into the Judean countryside and relaxed with them there. He was also baptizing.
MSG.23: At the same time, John was baptizing over at Aenon near Salim, where water was abundant.
MSG.24: This was before John was thrown into jail.
MSG.25: John's disciples got into an argument with the establishment Jews over the nature of baptism.
MSG.26: They came to John and said, "Rabbi, you know the one who was with you on the other side of the Jordan? The one you authorized with your witness? Well, he's now competing with us. He's baptizing, too, and everyone's going to him instead of us."
MSG.27: John answered, "It's not possible for a person to succeed—I'm talking about eternal success—without heaven's help.
MSG.28: You yourselves were there when I made it public that I was not the Messiah but simply the one sent ahead of him to get things ready.
MSG.29: The one who gets the bride is, by definition, the bridegroom. And the bridegroom's friend, his 'best man'—that's me—in place at his side where he can hear every word, is genuinely happy. How could he be jealous when he knows that the wedding is finished and the marriage is off to a good start? "That's why my cup is running over.
MSG.30: This is the assigned moment for him to move into the center, while I slip off to the sidelines.
MSG.31: "The One who comes from above is head and shoulders over other messengers from God. The earthborn is earthbound and speaks earth language; the heavenborn is in a league of his own.
MSG.32: He sets out the evidence of what he saw and heard in heaven. No one wants to deal with these facts.
MSG.33: But anyone who examines this evidence will come to stake his life on this: that God himself is the truth.
MSG.34: "The One that God sent speaks God's words. And don't think he rations out the Spirit in bits and pieces.
MSG.35: The Father loves the Son extravagantly. He turned everything over to him so he could give it away—a lavish distribution of gifts.
MSG.36: That is why whoever accepts and trusts the Son gets in on everything, life complete and forever! And that is also why the person who avoids and distrusts the Son is in the dark and doesn't see life. All he experiences of God is darkness, and an angry darkness at that."

NIV.1:
Jesus Teaches Nicodemus
Now there was a man of the Pharisees named Nicodemus, a member of the Jewish ruling council.
NIV.2: He came to Jesus at night and said, “Rabbi, we know you are a teacher who has come from God. For no one could perform the miraculous signs you are doing if God were not with him.”
NIV.3: In reply Jesus declared, “I tell you the truth, no one can see the kingdom of God unless he is born again.”
NIV.4: “How can a man be born when he is old?” Nicodemus asked. “Surely he cannot enter a second time into his mother’s womb to be born!”
NIV.5: Jesus answered, “I tell you the truth, no one can enter the kingdom of God unless he is born of water and the Spirit.
NIV.6: Flesh gives birth to flesh, but the Spirit gives birth to spirit.
NIV.7: You should not be surprised at my saying, ‘You must be born again.’
NIV.8: The wind blows wherever it pleases. You hear its sound, but you cannot tell where it comes from or where it is going. So it is with everyone born of the Spirit.”
NIV.10: “You are Israel’s teacher,” said Jesus, “and do you not understand these things?
NIV.11: I tell you the truth, we speak of what we know, and we testify to what we have seen, but still you people do not accept our testimony.
NIV.12: I have spoken to you of earthly things and you do not believe; how then will you believe if I speak of heavenly things?
NIV.13: No one has ever gone into heaven except the one who came from heaven—the Son of Man.
NIV.14: Just as Moses lifted up the snake in the desert, so the Son of Man must be lifted up,
NIV.15: that everyone who believes in him may have eternal life.
NIV.16: “For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him shall not perish but have eternal life.
NIV.17: For God did not send his Son into the world to condemn the world, but to save the world through him.
NIV.18: Whoever believes in him is not condemned, but whoever does not believe stands condemned already because he has not believed in the name of God’s one and only Son.
NIV.19: This is the verdict: Light has come into the world, but men loved darkness instead of light because their deeds were evil.
NIV.20: Everyone who does evil hates the light, and will not come into the light for fear that his deeds will be exposed.
NIV.21: But whoever lives by the truth comes into the light, so that it may be seen plainly that what he has done has been done through God.”
NIV.22:
John the Baptist’s Testimony About Jesus
After this, Jesus and his disciples went out into the Judean countryside, where he spent some time with them, and baptized.
NIV.23: Now John also was baptizing at Aenon near Salim, because there was plenty of water, and people were constantly coming to be baptized.
NIV.24: (This was before John was put in prison.)
NIV.25: An argument developed between some of John’s disciples and a certain Jew over the matter of ceremonial washing.
NIV.26: They came to John and said to him, “Rabbi, that man who was with you on the other side of the Jordan—the one you testified about—well, he is baptizing, and everyone is going to him.”
NIV.27: To this John replied, “A man can receive only what is given him from heaven.
NIV.28: You yourselves can testify that I said, ‘I am not the Christ but am sent ahead of him.’
NIV.29: The bride belongs to the bridegroom. The friend who attends the bridegroom waits and listens for him, and is full of joy when he hears the bridegroom’s voice. That joy is mine, and it is now complete.
NIV.30: He must become greater; I must become less.
NIV.31: “The one who comes from above is above all; the one who is from the earth belongs to the earth, and speaks as one from the earth. The one who comes from heaven is above all.
NIV.32: He testifies to what he has seen and heard, but no one accepts his testimony.
NIV.33: The man who has accepted it has certified that God is truthful.
NIV.34: For the one whom God has sent speaks the words of God, for God gives the Spirit without limit.
NIV.35: The Father loves the Son and has placed everything in his hands.
NIV.36: Whoever believes in the Son has eternal life, but whoever rejects the Son will not see life, for God’s wrath remains on him.”

NRSVCE.1: Now there was a Pharisee named Nicodemus, a leader of the Jews.
NRSVCE.2: He came to Jesus by night and said to him, “Rabbi, we know that you are a teacher who has come from God; for no one can do these signs that you do apart from the presence of God.”
NRSVCE.3: Jesus answered him, “Very truly, I tell you, no one can see the kingdom of God without being born from above.”
NRSVCE.4: Nicodemus said to him, “How can anyone be born after having grown old? Can one enter a second time into the mother’s womb and be born?”
NRSVCE.5: Jesus answered, “Very truly, I tell you, no one can enter the kingdom of God without being born of water and Spirit.
NRSVCE.6: What is born of the flesh is flesh, and what is born of the Spirit is spirit.
NRSVCE.7: Do not be astonished that I said to you, ‘You must be born from above.’
NRSVCE.8: The wind blows where it chooses, and you hear the sound of it, but you do not know where it comes from or where it goes. So it is with everyone who is born of the Spirit.”
NRSVCE.9: Nicodemus said to him, “How can these things be?”
NRSVCE.10: Jesus answered him, “Are you a teacher of Israel, and yet you do not understand these things?
NRSVCE.11: “Very truly, I tell you, we speak of what we know and testify to what we have seen; yet you do not receive our testimony.
NRSVCE.12: If I have told you about earthly things and you do not believe, how can you believe if I tell you about heavenly things?
NRSVCE.13: No one has ascended into heaven except the one who descended from heaven, the Son of Man.
NRSVCE.14: And just as Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, so must the Son of Man be lifted up,
NRSVCE.15: that whoever believes in him may have eternal life.
NRSVCE.16: “For God so loved the world that he gave his only Son, so that everyone who believes in him may not perish but may have eternal life.
NRSVCE.17: “Indeed, God did not send the Son into the world to condemn the world, but in order that the world might be saved through him.
NRSVCE.18: Those who believe in him are not condemned; but those who do not believe are condemned already, because they have not believed in the name of the only Son of God.
NRSVCE.19: And this is the judgment, that the light has come into the world, and people loved darkness rather than light because their deeds were evil.
NRSVCE.20: For all who do evil hate the light and do not come to the light, so that their deeds may not be exposed.
NRSVCE.21: But those who do what is true come to the light, so that it may be clearly seen that their deeds have been done in God.”
NRSVCE.22: After this Jesus and his disciples went into the Judean countryside, and he spent some time there with them and baptized.
NRSVCE.23: John also was baptizing at Aenon near Salim because water was abundant there; and people kept coming and were being baptized
NRSVCE.24: — John, of course, had not yet been thrown into prison.
NRSVCE.25: Now a discussion about purification arose between John’s disciples and a Jew.
NRSVCE.26: They came to John and said to him, “Rabbi, the one who was with you across the Jordan, to whom you testified, here he is baptizing, and all are going to him.”
NRSVCE.27: John answered, “No one can receive anything except what has been given from heaven.
NRSVCE.28: You yourselves are my witnesses that I said, ‘I am not the Messiah, but I have been sent ahead of him.’
NRSVCE.29: He who has the bride is the bridegroom. The friend of the bridegroom, who stands and hears him, rejoices greatly at the bridegroom’s voice. For this reason my joy has been fulfilled.
NRSVCE.30: He must increase, but I must decrease.”
NRSVCE.31: The one who comes from above is above all; the one who is of the earth belongs to the earth and speaks about earthly things. The one who comes from heaven is above all.
NRSVCE.32: He testifies to what he has seen and heard, yet no one accepts his testimony.
NRSVCE.33: Whoever has accepted his testimony has certified this, that God is true.
NRSVCE.34: He whom God has sent speaks the words of God, for he gives the Spirit without measure.
NRSVCE.35: The Father loves the Son and has placed all things in his hands.
NRSVCE.36: Whoever believes in the Son has eternal life; whoever disobeys the Son will not see life, but must endure God’s wrath.

NET.1: Now a certain man, a Pharisee named Nicodemus, who was a member of the Jewish ruling council,
NET.2: came to Jesus at night and said to him, “Rabbi, we know that you are a teacher who has come from God. For no one could perform the miraculous signs that you do unless God is with him.”
NET.3: Jesus replied, “I tell you the solemn truth, unless a person is born from above, he cannot see the kingdom of God.”
NET.4: Nicodemus said to him, “How can a man be born when he is old? He cannot enter his mother’s womb and be born a second time, can he?”
NET.5: Jesus answered, “I tell you the solemn truth, unless a person is born of water and spirit, he cannot enter the kingdom of God.
NET.6: What is born of the flesh is flesh, and what is born of the Spirit is spirit.
NET.7: Do not be amazed that I said to you, ‘You must all be born from above.’
NET.8: The wind blows wherever it will, and you hear the sound it makes, but do not know where it comes from and where it is going. So it is with everyone who is born of the Spirit.”
NET.9: Nicodemus replied, “How can these things be?”
NET.10: Jesus answered, “Are you the teacher of Israel and yet you don’t understand these things?
NET.11: I tell you the solemn truth, we speak about what we know and testify about what we have seen, but you people do not accept our testimony.
NET.12: If I have told you people about earthly things and you don’t believe, how will you believe if I tell you about heavenly things?
NET.13: No one has ascended into heaven except the one who descended from heaven — the Son of Man.
NET.14: Just as Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, so must the Son of Man be lifted up,
NET.15: so that everyone who believes in him may have eternal life.”
NET.16: For this is the way God loved the world: He gave his one and only Son, so that everyone who believes in him will not perish but have eternal life.
NET.17: For God did not send his Son into the world to condemn the world, but that the world should be saved through him.
NET.18: The one who believes in him is not condemned. The one who does not believe has been condemned already, because he has not believed in the name of the one and only Son of God.
NET.19: Now this is the basis for judging: that the light has come into the world and people loved the darkness rather than the light, because their deeds were evil.
NET.20: For everyone who does evil deeds hates the light and does not come to the light, so that their deeds will not be exposed.
NET.21: But the one who practices the truth comes to the light, so that it may be plainly evident that his deeds have been done in God.
NET.22: After this, Jesus and his disciples came into Judean territory, and there he spent time with them and was baptizing.
NET.23: John was also baptizing at Aenon near Salim, because water was plentiful there, and people were coming to him and being baptized.
NET.24: (For John had not yet been thrown into prison.)
NET.25: Now a dispute came about between some of John’s disciples and a certain Jew concerning ceremonial washing.
NET.26: So they came to John and said to him, “Rabbi, the one who was with you on the other side of the Jordan River, about whom you testified — see, he is baptizing, and everyone is flocking to him!”
NET.27: John replied, “No one can receive anything unless it has been given to him from heaven.
NET.28: You yourselves can testify that I said, ‘I am not the Christ,’ but rather, ‘I have been sent before him.’
NET.29: The one who has the bride is the bridegroom. The friend of the bridegroom, who stands by and listens for him, rejoices greatly when he hears the bridegroom’s voice. This then is my joy, and it is complete.
NET.30: He must become more important while I become less important.”
NET.31: The one who comes from above is superior to all. The one who is from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks about earthly things. The one who comes from heaven is superior to all.
NET.32: He testifies about what he has seen and heard, but no one accepts his testimony.
NET.33: The one who has accepted his testimony has confirmed clearly that God is truthful.
NET.34: For the one whom God has sent speaks the words of God, for he does not give the Spirit sparingly.
NET.35: The Father loves the Son and has placed all things under his authority.
NET.36: The one who believes in the Son has eternal life. The one who rejects the Son will not see life, but God’s wrath remains on him.
